b = dot(oc, rd) = 0 + 0.5*(-0.0995) + (-5)(0.995) = -0.04975 - 4.975 = -5.025
c = dot(oc,oc) - r² = 0 + 0.25 + 25 - 1 = 24.25
h = b² - c = 25.25 - 24.25 = 1.0
t = -b - sqrt(h) = 5.025 - 1 = 4.025

hit_pos = cam + t*rd = (0, -0.5, -5) + 4.025*(0, -0.0995, 0.995) = (0, -0.5-0.400, -5+4.005) = (0, -0.9, -0.995)
normal = norm(hit_pos - sc) = norm((0, -0.9-(-1), -0.995-0)) = norm((0, 0.1, -0.995)) ≈ (0, 0.1, -0.995)

Light direction: norm(-1,1,1) = (-0.577, 0.577, 0.577)
dot(N, L) = 0 + 0.1*0.577 + (-0.995)*0.577 = 0.0577 - 0.574 = -0.516

diff = max(-0.516, 0) = 0! ✓

So the sphere center DOES have diff=0 with light (-1,1,1). This is consistent with the original image showing (51,10,10) = 0.2*(1,0.2,0.2)*255.
